title: Singapore to Impose Flight Levy to Boost SAF Use
content: Senior Minister of State for Transport and National Development, Ms. Sun Xueling, delivered a response speech at the Second Reading of the Civil Aviation Authority of Singapore (Amendment) Bill on the Introduction of SAF Levy. Singaporeâ€™s proposed SAF Levy (Sustainable Aviation Fuel Levy) is designed to share the cost of meeting the countryâ€™s 1 % SAF adoption target across all air transport users â€” passengers, cargo carriers, and general aviation â€” proportionate to their fuel consumption. For passenger flights, indicative amounts are estimated at about SGD 3 for short-haul, SGD 6 for medium-haul, and SGD 16 for long-haul (economy class direct flights). The levy framework will be announced later, with sufficient lead time for airlines and users to prepare. To protect Singaporeâ€™s competitiveness as an aviation hub, transit and transfer passengers (who donâ€™t begin or end their journey in Singapore) will not be charged the SAF Levy, thereby preserving Changiâ€™s appeal as a connecting hub. The levy will exclude certain flights (e.g. flight training, humanitarian, or charitable flights) which will be specified in legislation. To manage volatility in SAF costs, the levy rates will remain fixed over a defined period, even if the actual SAF premium fluctuates, providing cost certainty to airlines and passengers.
author: Analysts Account
